title: Senior Production Manager
description:
About our group:

The Production Operations organization at Seagate Singapore (W1) is responsible for delivering safe, high‑quality, and cost‑efficient backend HDD manufacturing output. The team oversees 24/7 factory operations, workforce planning, operational excellence, production control, cross‑functional coordination, and continuous improvement initiatives to support Seagate’s global supply chain and customer commitments.

About the role - you will:

  • Lead end‑to‑end production operations across multiple manufacturing modules, ensuring stable output, quality, and on‑time delivery.
  • Drive factory KPIs including throughput, cycle time, yield, labor efficiency, WIP health, and operational cost.
  • Oversee shift operations, daily performance management, escalation handling, and cross‑functional issue resolution.
  • Partner closely with Engineering, IE, Quality, Supply Chain, and Maintenance teams to improve equipment uptime, reduce defects, and optimize workflow.
  • Lead workforce strategy: staffing plans, operator competency development, training matrices, and certification standards.
  • Implement Lean/6S/Kaizen practices to enhance productivity, workplace organization, and safety.
  • Support NPI builds, customer audits, and volume‑ramp readiness.
  • Champion safety culture—drive adherence to EHS standards, incident prevention, and safe work practices.
  • Develop frontline leaders (shift supervisors, line leads) through coaching, performance feedback, and career development.
  • Manage operational budgets, manpower forecasts, and cost‑reduction programs.

About you:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Manufacturing, Industrial Engineering, Operations Management, or related field.
  • Strong leader with proven ability to manage large operational teams in a fast‑paced environment.
  • Excellent communicator who can influence across levels and functions.
  • Data‑driven mindset with strong analytical and decision‑making capabilities.
  • Highly disciplined, organized, and focused on execution excellence.
  • Passionate about people development, teamwork, and continuous improvement.
  • Open to travel to Thailand for 1 month for on-job-training. 

Your experience includes:

  • 10+ years of experience in high‑volume manufacturing, with at least 5 years in operations leadership.
  • Demonstrated success in driving productivity, quality improvement, and cost‑efficiency.
  • Experience managing large teams (50–300+ staff) in shift or 24/7 operations.
  • Strong understanding of Lean manufacturing, SPC, 6S, and operational excellence frameworks.
  • Experience in HDD, semiconductor, electronics, or precision‑manufacturing environments.
  • Exposure to automation, MES systems, digital operations, or Industry 4.0 initiatives.
  • Background in ramp management, NPI builds, or customer‑facing operations.

postedCompany.description:
With more than four decades of storage innovation, Seagate empowers humanity to thrive in the data age and helps people and businesses navigate the ever-expanding data landscape. 

We craft precision-engineered, cutting-edge solutions that help the world store and manage exponential data growth.

Seagate is powered by our talented and passionate workforce of 29,000 employees across the globe who embody our core values: integrity, innovation, and inclusion. Striving towards excellence every single day, we show up with these values for our customers, business partners, shareholders, and communities alike.

Join us and get inspired to make a difference in the datasphere!

Seagate is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, genetic information, marital status, sex (which includes pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, religion, military and veteran status, or other status protected by applicable law.  We will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. EEO Know Your Rights Poster 

Seagate will provide reasonable accommodation with the application process upon request as required to comply with applicable laws. If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at accommodations@seagate.com. 

All Seagate jobs will remain open for a minimum of seven days.

For information on how Seagate collects and uses your personal information during the application process, please review the Applicant Privacy Statement.
